isolation.sharedState: none — tests are pure functions on in-memory structures

assertions.title: Rounded rect with zero radius equals fill_rect (byte-compare output)

steps.expected: All 1606 tests pass
Test passes — byte-identical output
Test passes — byte-identical output with blending
Test creates fill_rect and fill_rounded_rect(radius=0), asserts byte equality
steps.observed: Confirmed: creates ref_buf with fill_rect and rr_buf with fill_rounded_rect(radius=0), asserts assert_eq!(ref_buf, rr_buf). Blend variant does same with fill_rect_blend vs fill_rounded_rect_blend(radius=0) over colored background.

evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs lines 6272-6321: byte-compare framebuffer output for both opaque and blended variants

assertions.title: Rounded rect with small radius has anti-aliased corners (100x50, corner_radius=4)

steps.expected: Test passes
Test samples corner and interior pixels
steps.observed: Confirmed: renders 100x50 red rect on black with corner_radius=4. Asserts interior pixel (50,30) is fully opaque red. Asserts pixel inside corner arc (15,10) is opaque red. Asserts exact corner pixel (10,5) is NOT fully solid red (i.e., anti-aliased blend).

evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs lines 6324-6353: verifies interior fully opaque, corner pixel anti-aliased (partial coverage)

assertions.title: Rounded rect radius clamped to half-dimension (40x20, corner_radius=255 → capsule)

steps.expected: Test passes
Test compares oversized radius to clamped render
steps.observed: Confirmed: renders 40x20 rect with radius=255 and another with radius=10 (min(40,20)/2). Asserts buf1==buf2 (byte-identical), proving clamping.

evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs lines 6354-6377: byte-compare oversized radius=255 vs explicit radius=10 (min(w,h)/2)

assertions.title: Rounded rect anti-aliasing uses gamma-correct blending

steps.expected: Test passes
Test verifies gamma-correct blending at edge pixels
steps.observed: Confirmed: renders red rounded rect (radius=10) on blue background using fill_rounded_rect_blend. Walks corner pixels to find partially-covered AA pixel (one with both r>10 and b>10). Asserts at least one AA pixel exists. Comment documents sRGB math expectation: gamma-correct values are 'heavier' than naive linear.

evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs lines 6378-6435: red on blue blend, finds AA edge pixel with partial coverage, validates gamma-correct compositing

assertions.title: NEON SIMD rounded rect matches scalar (byte-compare)

steps.expected: Test passes
Test passes
Test passes
Tests compare NEON paths vs scalar/fill_rect
steps.observed: Confirmed: (1) 200x100 rounded rect with radius=16, interior rows byte-compared against fill_rect — ensures NEON fast-path for interior matches scalar. (2) Same for blend variant. (3) Various widths [5,6,7,8,9,13,16,17,100] exercise aligned and unaligned NEON 4-pixel chunks, verifying center pixels.

evidence.testOutput:
test neon_rounded_rect_interior_matches_fill_rect ... ok
test neon_rounded_rect_blend_interior_matches_fill_rect_blend ... ok
test neon_rounded_rect_various_widths ... ok
evidence.sourceVerification: neon.rs lines 949-1039: byte-compare NEON interior rows vs fill_rect for opaque and blend; various widths for alignment edge cases

steps.expected: Test passes
Test verifies child pixels clipped at rounded boundary
steps.observed: Confirmed: parent 100x100 with corner_radius=20, CLIPS_CHILDREN flag. Child fills entire parent in green. Renders scene. Asserts interior (50,50) is green (child visible). Asserts corner (0,0) green channel != 255 (child clipped by rounded parent). Also checks (1,1) — well outside radius=20 arc.

evidence.sourceVerification: scene_render.rs lines 1371-1427: parent corner_radius=20 + CLIPS_CHILDREN, child covers entire area, corner pixels verify clipping

steps.expected: Test passes
Test renders triangle path, verifies interior/exterior pixels
steps.observed: Confirmed: creates path with MoveTo(10,80), LineTo(50,10), LineTo(90,80), Close(). Red fill, transparent stroke. Renders via scene graph. Asserts center (50,50) has r>200 (red fill). Asserts exterior (5,5) has r<10 (black background).

evidence.sourceVerification: scene_render.rs lines 1728-1765: MoveTo/LineTo/Close triangle, interior red fill verified, exterior black verified

assertions.title: CurveTo cubic bezier renders smooth curves

steps.expected: Test passes
Test verifies curve renders filled pixels at expected points
steps.observed: Confirmed: creates path with MoveTo(10,90), CurveTo(10,10,90,10,90,90), Close(). Green fill. Control points at y=10 cause curve to bulge upward. Asserts interior (50,70) has g>200 (green fill inside curve). Asserts (50,88) also green (between closing line and curve). This proves smooth curve rendering, not straight-line approximation.

evidence.sourceVerification: scene_render.rs lines 1766-1812: CurveTo with control points creating arc, interior points verified as filled

steps.expected: Test passes — intermediate alpha values found
Test passes — blended pixels found
Test passes — steep diagonal also has AA
Tests verify coverage-based AA with intermediate alpha
steps.observed: Confirmed: (1) diagonal_has_smooth_edges draws (0,2)→(19,10), scans for pixels with 0<alpha<255, asserts both full and intermediate alpha exist. (2) blends_with_background draws white line on gray(100,100,100), finds pixels that are neither pure bg nor pure white (blended). (3) steep_diagonal_smooth draws (2,0)→(8,29), verifies intermediate alpha on steep lines too.

evidence.testOutput:
test draw_line_aa_diagonal_has_smooth_edges ... ok
test draw_line_aa_blends_with_background ... ok
test draw_line_aa_steep_diagonal_smooth ... ok
evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs lines 963-1148: three tests verify coverage-based AA (intermediate alpha, background blending, steep angles)

assertions.title: AA lines at axis-aligned angles are pixel-perfect

steps.expected: Test passes — no AA fringe
Test passes — no AA fringe
Tests verify only expected pixels set, no unnecessary AA
steps.observed: Confirmed: (1) horizontal_pixel_perfect draws (1,2)→(8,2), asserts every pixel on row 2 from x=1..8 is fully opaque white, rows above (y=1) and below (y=3) have alpha=0. (2) vertical_pixel_perfect draws (2,1)→(2,8), asserts every pixel in column 2 from y=1..8 is fully opaque white, columns left (x=1) and right (x=3) have alpha=0.

evidence.testOutput:
test draw_line_aa_horizontal_pixel_perfect ... ok
test draw_line_aa_vertical_pixel_perfect ... ok
evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs lines 920-968: horizontal and vertical lines verified crisp with zero AA fringe on neighboring rows/columns

assertions.title: No performance regression for sharp corners (corner_radius=0 → early branch to fill_rect)

steps.expected: Test passes — identical output to fill_rect
Test passes — byte-identical
Test passes — compositor uses rect path
Tests prove early branch to fill_rect for radius=0
steps.observed: Confirmed: (1) rounded_rect_zero_radius_no_overhead uses large 256x128 surface (exercises NEON), byte-compares fill_rect vs fill_rounded_rect(radius=0). Identical output proves early branch. (2) corner_radius_zero_falls_back_to_rect renders scene with corner_radius=0, verifies corner pixel (0,0) is fully green (sharp, no rounding applied).

evidence.testOutput:
test rounded_rect_zero_radius_no_overhead ... ok
test rounded_rect_zero_radius_equals_fill_rect ... ok
test corner_radius_zero_falls_back_to_rect ... ok
evidence.sourceVerification: drawing.rs:6451-6470 (large surface byte-compare), scene_render.rs:1335-1370 (compositor sharp corner verification)

summary: Tested 11 assertions: 11 passed, 0 failed. All 1606 tests in the full suite pass. Each assertion's mapped tests were run individually and confirmed passing. Test source code was read and verified to check the claimed property from each assertion.
